Ad Text:
Revenue Operations Lead

Sydney | Hybrid | Full-time




About Protecht

Protecht is a global risk management SaaS business. We help organizations manage risk better every day. We’re growing fast, scaling globally, and building teams that are smart, ambitious and supportive.




The role (and why it matters?)

We’re looking for a Revenue Operations Lead, to join our Sydney team. Reporting to the Global Head of Revenue Operations, you’ll own and optimize core revenue processes, systems, and data integrity across Sales, Customer Success, and Partnerships, whilst working very closely with Marketing Operations. You will drive sustainable growth across APAC, the UK and the US.

This is a visible, hands-on, and high-impact role with strong exposure to senior leaders and global teams. You’ll have real influence, autonomy and room to grow.




Why join us?

We’re proud to be certified as a Great Place to Work four years in a row. We look after our people while delivering at a high level. Here are some of the perks of working with Protecht:

Genuine career growth in a fast-growing SaaS business.

Flexible work that supports work and life - our way of working lets people work across home and in our offices.

A strong commitment to your learning and development - fortnightly dedicated L&D afternoons.

A strong focus on work / life balance with access to Birthday leave, bonus days, paid parental leave and long service leave.

Modern Sydney CBD office.

Global exposure across APAC, UK, and US teams.

Award-winning culture – Great Place to Work (4 years running).

 

About you!

You’re comfortable working at pace and dealing with complexity. You care about outcomes, collaboration and doing things properly. And you want to be part of a business that’s going places. This role suits you if you have solid experience in SaaS sales cycles and a strong interest in data, systems, and continuous process improvement.




What you’ll be doing

You’ll collaborate closely with teams across Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, Finance and Business Systems to enhance performance, improve operational efficiency, and provide the insights needed to support and drive growth. Some of your key responsibilities will be:




Go-to-Market Tech Stack

Own the configuration and long-term roadmap of core GTM systems.

Maintain system governance, data security, user access, and compliance.

Develop scalable system architectures and integrations to support multi-region expansion.

Evaluate and implement new tools.

 Revenue Process & Architecture

Design, document, and continuously improve revenue workflows (lead lifecycle, routing, scoring, opportunity management, handoffs, renewals).

Create automation to reduce manual work and increase operational efficiency.

Collaborate with GTM leaders to ensure processes reflect best practices and business strategy.

 Data Quality, Insights & Reporting Infrastructure

Enforce CRM data quality standards through hygiene processes, validation rules, and governance.

Partner with Head of Revenue Ops and analyst to support accurate forecasting, pipeline visibility, and leadership reporting.

Ensure consistent GTM metric definitions across the organization.

 Cross-Functional Partnership

Serve as the primary systems partner for Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success.

Translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions.

Collaborate closely with Finance, IT, and Product teams on revenue-impacting initiatives (usage data, billing, product integrations).

 Enablement & Support

Training the global GTM teams on system capabilities, workflows, and new features.

Develop documentation, SOPs, and change management plans.

Handle escalations and manage system support queues.




You will be a great fit if you have:

4 years’ + experience in Revenue Operations, Business Systems, or similar roles in a B2B SaaS environment.

Strong experience with CRM architecture (e.g., Salesforce, HubSpot).

Understanding of system integrations, APIs, and middleware (e.g. Workato, Zapier, etc.).

Familiarity with sales engagement and call intelligence platforms (e.g. Apollo, Gong, Outreach, Clari, or similar GTM platforms).

Experience supporting multi-region revenue teams.

Analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into insights and action.

Strong attention to detail and a process-oriented approach to problem-solving.

Familiarity with revenue forecasting processes and data modelling.

Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate cross-functionally.

Personal drive to improve current skillsets or expand into new domain areas.

Comfortable working in a high-velocity environment with competing priorities.

Excellent communication skills and ability to work with stakeholders across functions and regions.




Interested?

If you think this may be your next opportunity and you want to be part of a Great Place to Work™ – Certified organization, Apply online today!

Visit our website https://www.protechtgroup.com/ to find out a little more about working with us.




Protecht is an equal opportunity employer. We value diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for everyone.

We are seeking an experienced Senior Project Manager to lead a portfolio of high-impact transformation initiatives within a growing, purpose-driven organisation.

This is a hands-on delivery role where you will operate at the intersection of business and technology, driving multiple concurrent initiatives across data, systems, and operational uplift.

The Opportunity

You will take ownership of a diverse program of work focused on modernising platforms, improving reporting capability, and enabling better portfolio and asset management outcomes. Reporting directly to the COO, with exposure to senior stakeholders, you will play a critical role in shaping and delivering key business initiatives.

Key Responsibilities

Lead end-to-end delivery of a portfolio of transformation initiatives
Establish operational and external reporting frameworks, enabling self-service through a common data platform
Deliver enhanced reporting solutions, including improved mechanisms for external stakeholders to provide project and safety updates
Drive enhancements to Dynamics 365, including user experience improvements
Lead the transition from legacy tools into D365, including platform rationalisation and decommissioning
Oversee delivery of an asset/portfolio management solution, potentially integrating multiple systems via an orchestration layer
Support data remediation and migration from spreadsheet-based processes into structured platforms
Identify and deliver practical AI use cases to improve efficiency and insight
Act as the key interface between business stakeholders and technology teams

What You Bring

Proven experience delivering complex, multi-stream transformation programs
Strong background in Microsoft Dynamics 365 environments
Experience implementing portfolio or asset management systems (highly regarded)
Demonstrated ability to manage both business and technology stakeholders
Strong data and reporting program experience
Comfortable operating in environments transitioning from manual/spreadsheet processes to structured platforms
Ability to step into light change management responsibilities, including communications, stakeholder engagement, and training delivery
Excellent stakeholder management skills, including C-suite engagement

Contract Details

Initial 6-month contract with strong likelihood of extension
Immediate start preferred
High-impact role with significant visibility across the organisation

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high‑performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.

Your New Role (12‑month contract)

As the Manager, Service Improvement Optimisation, you will play a critical role in building the foundations of a newly established function focused on delivering measurable, sustainable improvements to the customer and colleague experience.

This is a hands-on role that requires someone who can bring structure, clarity, and delivery discipline to how we identify, prioritise, and embed change. You'll be responsible for designing and implementing frameworks, tools, and reporting that enable scalable, high-quality service delivery and continuous improvement.

You'll also play a key role in supporting and guiding others across the team, coaching in delivery disciplines, shaping repeatable templates and workflows, and embedding governance rhythms that promote consistency, transparency, and uplift in capability.

You will collaborate with other change and improvement teams to design and maintain a common dashboard, pipeline of work, reporting suite, and connection framework, ensuring alignment, transparency, and shared success.

You will drive robust reporting and insights, support change engagement, and enable continuous improvement by providing updates, surfacing opportunities, and supporting Service Delivery teams to deliver, refer, or shape improvements through the right channels.

Key responsibilities include:

Designing, embedding, and continuously improving Service Improvement frameworks, processes, templates, and tools.
Reviewing and prioritising incoming work to ensure alignment with strategic objectives and effective resource utilisation.
Owning and managing the intake process to identify, assess, and prioritise improvement opportunities from data, insights, and stakeholder feedback, in partnership with the broader Service Improvement team.
Leading team‑level reporting, including progress tracking and insights that inform decision‑
Facilitating change engagement and communication to ensure initiatives are understood, embedded, and transitioned into BAU seamlessly.
Building collaborative relationships across Service Delivery, Member Group, the broader organisation, and external vendors/partners to drive aligned, cross‑functional delivery.
Collaborating with other change and improvement teams to design and maintain a shared dashboard, reporting suite, and pipeline of work that enables transparency, alignment, and shared success.
Analysing data and insights to identify service pain points and improvement opportunities.
Driving improvements that uplift the health, performance, and scalability of Service Delivery teams, enabling more efficient and effective service to members, advisers, and employers.
Supporting the delivery of key programs and initiatives, from inception to completion including scoping, planning, risk management, execution, benefits tracking and post‑implementation review.
Support the Senior Manager, Service Improvement with co-creating team vision and strategy, and providing meaningful feedback.

What You'll Need

Minimum 8-10 years' experience in service improvement, operations, or service delivery, with a strong track record of designing and embedding frameworks, tools, and delivery rhythms from the ground up.
Proven experience in program or project management, with the ability to lead, coach, and uplift capability in delivery disciplines that are fit-for-purpose and adaptable to business needs.
Strong understanding of world-class service excellence principles and how to embed them into operational practices.
Demonstrated experience designing and delivering dashboards, reporting suites, and performance insights that inform decision-making and clearly demonstrate impact and benefits achieved.
Strong analytical, problem‑solving and data‑driven decision‑making skills.
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with a collaborative, people-centred approach.
Experience in change management and delivering clear, effective change communications.
Proficiency in Microsoft Teams, Excel, Word, PowerPoint, and project management tools.
Familiarity with Microsoft Power Platform tools (desirable).
Experience in superannuation, financial advice, or corporate superannuation (desirable).

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching, and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness, and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit, and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results.

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy.

https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate‑privacy‑notice

Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

Ad Text:

Ad Text:
Work.

Are you a strategic thinker with a passion for project excellence? Join Sunwater as a Senior Governance and Assurance Advisor, where you’ll play a vital role in the practice of Governance and Assurance across projects, programs and portfolio management (P3M) in the Technology Portfolio, ensuring decision makers have confidence that the project, programs and portfolios will deliver value and allowing them to make informed investment, planning and delivery decisions for Sunwater. You will provide technical advice in the application of best practice principles within the project, program and portfolio management framework and integrated controls.  

As a Senior Governance and Assurance Advisor at Sunwater, you will:

Deliver Level 2 Governance and Assurance activities including Project Health Checks, Gate Reviews and facilitating independent Peer/Technical reviews as required and in collaboration with Project Sponsors and Portfolio Managers
Provide expert guidance and strategic advice to Project Managers, Sponsors, and Portfolio Managers on planning and delivery, ensuring compliance with ePMO Procedures (P3MF, Assurance, Controls) and related systems.
Develop and lead P3M governance and assurance data and reporting, including assurance and decision registers and monthly ePMO assurance reporting to Portfolio Committees
Design and support the assurance forward program, including the coordination of gate reviewers, in alignment with strategic priorities and ensuring timely delivery of assurance activities

We are seeking a talented individual with a proven track record in ePMO planning and execution or similar experience in technology project management. You will hold tertiary qualifications in project management or similar, and have experience in a similar role. You will also bring:

P3M expertise: Experience in designing, implementing and maintaining P3M tools and templates to maximise value and ensure project excellence
Strategic vision: Ability to design and deliver project, program, portfolio improvement strategies and programs
Communication skills: Excellent communication skills with a knack for writing and presenting quality reports and findings to senior leadership, including the Board and executive leadership team

This role offers an array of attractive benefits including:

Base salary starting $145,346.26
12.75% superannuation
Access to banked time and TOIL
17.5% leave loading
A genuine emphasis on work/life balance

Water.

Imagine a career where your work truly matters, where you are part of a team that values you and your contributions. At Sunwater, you will bring life’s most essential resource to over 4,500 agriculture, urban and industrial customers, helping to sustain communities and deliver water for prosperity across regional Queensland. We share the values of these communities — starting with caring about our people. Engage in fascinating work that is constantly evolving, allowing you to grow and shape a successful career and develop unique skills and experience in an industry brimming with opportunity.

Life.

At Sunwater, we believe our employees are our greatest asset, and we are committed to providing the support, resources and benefits that you need to thrive. That's why we offer a range of attractive benefits designed to help you bring your best every day, including:

My benefits: Access employee benefits and discounts hosted by Reward Gateway
My wellbeing: Prioritise your wellbeing with our comprehensive health and wellness offerings and programs
My time: Enjoy flexible working arrangements and the ability to purchase up to 4 additional weeks of leave, ensuring you can tailor your work to fit your lifestyle
My growth: We prioritise your professional growth with career development programs, study assistance, support for professional memberships, and structured early career pathways to help you build a rewarding and successful career

Now is the perfect time to join Sunwater and take advantage of the boundless opportunities for your career today and in the future.  We are a diverse team and proud of the unique journeys and stories each of us brings. As we continue to grow, we need more diverse backgrounds and perspectives than ever before. We are committed to a barrier-free recruitment process and work environment. If you require any accommodations, please let us know so we can ensure you can fully participate in our recruitment experience, which includes reference verification, psychometric testing, background checks and a pre-employment medical evaluation to ensure the best fit for our team.

Sunwater acknowledges Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples as the first peoples of this country and Traditional Custodians of the land and water we rely on. We respect and value their continued sacred connection to Country, including the diverse, rich traditions, languages and customs that are the longest living in the world.

Apply for this role by hitting the Apply button now or visit our website to learn more.

Your New Role

Reporting to the Senior Manager, Risk Advisory within Group Risk, the Senior Risk Analyst is a key member of the Risk & Governance Advisory team (Line 2).

You will play a critical role in supporting Line 1 risk management activities across the Group Risk domain, helping ensure the right level of risk is taken to deliver on strategy. Working closely with business stakeholders, you will provide practical and tailored risk management solutions as the Fund responds to increased regulatory pressure, disruptive technologies, changing demographics and evolving member expectations. This is a 7‑month full‑time maximum‑term contract.

In this role, you'll:

- Partner with business stakeholders to manage risk in line with the Fund's Risk Management Framework

- Deliver business and material risk assessments, including identification and documentation of risks and controls

- Conduct control testing activities, including control self‑assessments and validation of control effectiveness

- Support incident and obligation management activities across the Group Risk domain

- Develop, monitor and maintain action plans and findings to address identified risks

- Contribute to risk reporting, dashboards and insights for Senior Management and Board Committees

- Promote continuous improvement and a strong, positive risk culture across Line 1 and Line 2 teams

What You'll Need

- Tertiary qualifications in Business, Commerce, Law or a related industry discipline (essential)

- Post‑tertiary qualifications in financial services, superannuation or risk management (desirable)

- Approximately 4+ years' experience in risk management and/or assurance, ideally within financial services or consulting

- Strong understanding of control design and operating effectiveness

- Demonstrated experience in risk assessments, control testing, incident and obligation management

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage, influence and respectfully challenge stakeholders

- Strong analytical and problem‑solving skills, with high attention to detail

- A collaborative mindset, resilience and a clear growth mindset

Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Apply now if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes, and would like the opportunity to help shape how one of the world's largest institutional investors tells its investment story.

Important Information

AustralianSuper uses AI to review resumes and conduct initial phone interviews. This involves processing your resume, cover letter, and voice recording in Australia and the USA to help assess your suitability for the applied role. When invited to an AI phone screening, you may opt for a traditional phone screening instead. For information on how we handle your personal data, please refer to our Candidate Privacy Policy:

Produce clear and concise documentation for code, processes, and workflows.

Senior Data Engineer - 12-Month Contract

Location: Sydney, NSW
Rate: $1,100 per day (inclusive of super)

About the Company

Join a high-performing Data Team within a leading global financial services organisation, working on a large-scale transformation initiative in the Banking and Financial Services division.

The organisation operates across multiple international markets and is known for its strong track record of innovation and sustained growth. You'll be part of a collaborative, inclusive environment where individuals are empowered to contribute ideas and drive meaningful outcomes.

About the Role

As a Senior Data Engineer, you will play a key role in delivering critical regulatory transformation initiatives. This is a hands-on position suited to an experienced engineer with strong expertise in data engineering, ETL/ELT pipelines, and modern data platforms.

You will contribute to enhancing platform efficiency through automation and AI-driven capabilities while collaborating closely with both technical and business stakeholders.

Key Responsibilities

Design, develop, and optimise complex SQL queries for large-scale data processing

Build and maintain robust ETL/ELT data pipelines

Implement and manage data integration solutions and data warehouse platforms

Collaborate with stakeholders to deliver data solutions aligned with regulatory and business requirements

Enhance platform performance through automation and AI-driven improvements

Ensure data quality, integrity, and governance standards are maintained

Contribute to data architecture design and best practices

Skills & Experience Required

Advanced expertise in SQL, including performance tuning and optimisation

Strong hands-on experience building code-based ETL/ELT pipelines

Proven experience with data integration tools and data warehousing solutions

Experience working in mature cloud environments

Solid understanding of data governance concepts (data lineage, metadata, data quality, reconciliation)

Knowledge of data architecture and data modelling techniques

Strong problem-solving skills and ability to work in complex environments

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Experience with Python and automation/AI capabilities is highly desirable

Prior experience in banking or financial services is preferred

If you feel you possess the relevant skills to the above, apply now with most updated CV!

Salary: No contractor fees / two pay runs per week
Ad Text:
Finance Analyst

Contract for 12 months | Brisbane CBD | Hybrid working

Brisbane based candidates only

We are partnering with a leading Queensland Government client to recruit an experienced Finance Analyst. In this role, you will be responsible for implementing and supporting the management and reporting of all financial aspects of the TDPS and it’s sub Portfolios, Programs and Project).

Key Responsibilities:

Reviewing existing Technology Delivery Portfolio financial models and implementing them in the TDPS standard format

Establishing reporting templates and mechanisms for consolidating the Technology Delivery Portfolio projects and programs for management reporting

Establishing the TDPS financial management cadence within Technology Delivery Portfolio and ensuring this is complied with by Program and Project Managers.

Month-end accounting process including Project and Program forecasts, complex contract financial forecast updates and variance analysis.

Financial reporting to various stakeholders including the CTEO, Heads of ENMP and TSD, Chief Financial Officer, ePMO and various Boards.

Maintenance and development of financial plans/budgets/forecasts including annual capital expenditure (CAPEX) versus operational expenditure (OPEX) plans.

Support management of the financial controls for the TDPS budget.

Assist with maintaining Work Break Down Structures in SAP

Process project forecast uploads in SAP

What We’re Looking For?

At least 4 –5 years’ experience in Financial Analysis or similar role, within a project delivery environment

Tertiary qualification in accounting, finance or related discipline, or equivalent experience

In-depth knowledge and experience in accounting practices, particularly with respect to budgeting, forecasting and reporting on large programs with budgets over $100m and experience with tracking Vendor contract payments.

Well-developed management abilities, including a proven ability to successfully manage a range of financial activities.

Why apply through Lime Recruitment?

Enjoy two pay runs per week without contractor fees. As a Queensland-owned agency, we offer personalised service and are a Tier 1 supplier to the Queensland Government. Awarded Australia’s top-performing small agency in 2021 and 2022, with a finalist spot in 2023. Partner with Lime for genuine, experienced, friendly recruiters dedicated to securing the best opportunities for you. Organisation Profile
Our client is a leading ASX listed and multinational giant in the FMCG sector. With a strong track record of growth through M&A and significant capital investment, the business is now focused on uplifting group-wide planning, performance, and decision-making capability.

Headquartered in Melbourne CBD, the organisation combines the scale and sophistication of a global group with a high-performing, collaborative head office finance function. This is a business undergoing continued transformation, creating a unique opportunity for a commercially minded finance professional to contribute to enterprise-level performance and decision-making.

Role Overview
This is a high-impact, group-level FP&A role focused on shaping how the organisation plans, measures, and drives performance.
You will play a key role in delivering group-wide budgeting and forecasting cycles, providing executive-level insights, and supporting the uplift of planning cadence, performance visibility, and decision-making across a complex, multi-entity environment.
Working closely with senior stakeholders, including the Group CFO and executive leadership team, this role offers significant exposure and the opportunity to influence outcomes beyond traditional reporting


Key responsibilities include:

Play a key role in delivering group-wide budgeting, forecasting, and long-range planning processes
Deliver executive-level reporting and performance insights for ELT and Board audiences
Leverage large, complex datasets to generate actionable insights and challenge performance outcomes
Support the preparation of board and investor materials, including performance commentary and analysis
Track and quantify financial and operational benefits across transformation and strategic initiatives
Partner with senior stakeholders across the business to influence performance and improve planning discipline
Contribute to the uplift of planning cadence, reporting frameworks, and financial models across the group
Drive continuous improvement initiatives across systems, tools, and processes
Ideal Candidate
You will bring a strong foundation in FP&A, group reporting, or commercial finance within a large, complex organisation. You are analytically strong, commercially curious, and confident engaging with senior stakeholders.

This role suits someone who enjoys operating at an enterprise level, not just reporting numbers, but driving performance outcomes and influencing decision-making.

Key Requirements

CA qualified (or equivalent)
Big 4 background (Audit, Advisory, or Transaction Services) highly desirable
Experience within a large corporate, ASX-listed, or multinational environment
Exposure to group FP&A, consolidation, or central finance functions highly regarded
Demonstrated involvement in budgeting, forecasting, and planning cycles
Experience delivering or contributing to executive or board-level reporting
Advanced Excel and strong financial modelling capability
Exposure to data visualisation tools (e.g. Power BI) highly regarded

Reporting to the Head of Risk & Compliance, this position is accountable for managing operational risks arising from operations within the Enterprise Services division. The Operational Risk Lead is responsible for ensuring the effectiveness, compliance, and integrity of activities across the Customer Care, Technology, and Marketing business units. This role involves executing risk management and assurance activities, ensuring adherence to company policies, legal obligations, and industry standards.

 

Let’s look at some key responsibilities:


Ensure adherence to Enterprise Risk Policies and frameworks by undertaking assurance activities and providing valuable insights and trends to appropriate governance forums.

Ensure effective maintenance of risk registers and profiles, including risk identification, execution of RCSA’s and resolution of self-identified, internal, and external audit issues.

Partner with Senior Managers to ensure business risks are identified and managed. Where risks have materialised (risk events/incidents), ensure they are captured and remediated efficiently, including conducting thorough root cause analysis.

Undertake independent testing of control portfolios to ensure compliance with relevant obligations and industry standards. Partner with stakeholders to address control gaps and implement operational efficiencies.

Provide high-quality risk reporting to business leadership and risk committees.

Drive continuous strategic initiatives that strengthen and enhance the risk management position of the Enterprise Services division, including problem resolution, event root cause remediation, and operational improvements.

Conduct detailed risk assessments and update frameworks and relevant policies and procedures to support regulatory/ process changes, outsourcing arrangements, and technology or business initiatives.

Develop and deliver the Line 1 assurance plan, ensuring alignment with higher-risk areas and regulatory obligations.

Establish and monitor Key Risk Indicators (KRIs); analyse trends and escalate breaches promptly.

Provide high-quality risk reporting to business leadership and risk committees.

Lead proactive assessments to identify process improvement opportunities, including streamlining, automation, and the design or enhancement of controls to strengthen resilience and efficiency.

Perform any other duties as may be reasonably required from time to time.




 We're looking for the following: 


Relevant tertiary qualifications or demonstrated equivalent knowledge through prior work experience.

5+ years’ experience in Operational Risk Management, Internal Audit, or Quality Assurance practices and programs.

Technology, Operations and Marketing experience banking or financial services environment, with a solid understanding of core processes workflows and customer journeys within the financial services industry.

Strong understanding of regulatory frameworks and compliance obligations relevant to consumer finance / financial services across Australia and New Zealand. 

Demonstrated expertise in control design, testing, and uplift initiatives including obligation registers and mapping.

Experience leading assurance reviews and thematic risk assessments to identify and address systemic issues.

Strong analytical, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills.

Excellent communication and report writing skills, with an ability to translate technical issues into business-friendly language.

Proficiency in interpreting complex process maps, data flows, system controls, and operational workflows.

Strong ability to collaborate with cross-functional teams, with excellent communication skills and the capability to influence and articulate complex risk scenarios.

Relevant Operational Risk Management experience within the Australian financial services industry.

Knowledge of, and experience with, risk management systems, including Protecht.







Sometimes the best candidates don’t have 100% of what is listed above, but if you have most and are confident, you’d be a good fit, we’d love to hear from you!

We always support a safe, healthy, engaging, and productive working environment for all employees and workers, whether that be in your home and office, or a combination of both.

Our QLD Government client is currently seeking a Data Analyst (Procurement) to be responsible for the analysis of procurement-related data, including financial and transactional services, analysis, strategies, technical advice, and support services.

As the Data Analyst (Procurement), you will:

Apply procurement (sourcing, operational procurement, contract management, performance, policy, systems) and category management fundamentals to deliver of meaningful business intelligence outputs.
Identify and use the most appropriate analytical tools, techniques, and systems to analyse and interpret a wide range of supplier and organisational information and develop reports, forecasts, and advice to inform and support procurement planning and decision-making.
Provide advice, supported by quality business intelligence to management, customers, and stakeholders, ensuring data and concepts are translated for the audience.
Support the development and implementation of best practice principles to business intelligence and data management with a focus on process, and data and document management.
Develop business intelligence and data management system requirements to meet operational and strategic objectives.
Coordinate and collaborate with key stakeholders to ensure fit for purpose systems implemented.
Lead and deliver procurement business intelligence and data management projects/outputs, applying business intelligence and data management tools such as Power BI, Excel, and AI.
Build and leverage stakeholder relationships to deliver outcomes.
What you’ll bring to the role:

Knowledge and experience in the management, extraction, analysis, and reporting (presentation and communication) of data and analysis findings
Demonstrated ability to acquire knowledge of procurement policies and procedures which contribute to procurement operational governance, reporting and improvement outcomes.
Demonstrated ability to utilise a range of software programs including but not limited to Microsoft Word, Microsoft Excel, data modelling systems, and database applications.
Demonstrate respect and appreciation for legislation and policy frameworks by consistently operating to key standards.
Maintain a high standard of practice through governance and risk management.
 
HOW TO APPLY:

Please submit your resume for consideration via the appropriate link. A cover letter demonstrating your relevant experience may also be reviewed.

Diversity, Equity & Inclusion at Hudson
Hudson is committed to helping you find a workplace where you feel respected, supported, and free to thrive. We welcome applications from all backgrounds, identities, and lived experiences—because when different voices come together, amazing things happen.

Casual Loading 
Please note for all Australian based contract and temporary roles only, the pay rate is inclusive of mandatory 25% casual loading. This excludes permanent and fixed term roles.

A bit about us  

If you are looking for a fantastic team and a challenging, fast, and dynamic work environment, you are at the right place!  

On our journey to making a positive difference across the world, you will work with passionate people in an innovative and collaborative organization as we inspire wellness through #PurposeLedCareers  

We are dedicated to our customers, community, and environment and are looking for similar-minded talent to join us! As an equal opportunity employer, we care deeply about creating an inclusive workplace where our team members feel valued, respected, and empowered.  

Our total rewards package offering includes additional annual leave, bonuses, wellness programs, health benefits, flexible and remote working arrangements, and performance awards - not to mention various social and recreational activities, all of which are regional specific.  

With over 3,400 team members working across the globe, our vision is focused on premium nutrition and lifetime wellness, backed by science. We have 3 business segments - Baby, Adult and Pet Nutrition and Care - supporting whole - family health and happiness across 7 international brands.




What this means for you 

The variety of work creates unlimited opportunities to excel across a breadth of disciplines; you will be presented with a wealth of new pathways and opportunities to explore throughout your career. Working with us will be more than just a job; we guarantee a life-changing experience as you step into an environment that moves quickly and presents new challenges daily.




Your role within the team 

As the Reward and People Insights Manager, you will shape and drive employee rewards and compensation strategies across ANZ. This high-impact role involves designing and managing total rewards programs - including salary, bonuses, benefits, incentives, and recognition - ensuring they are competitive, aligned with business objectives, and reflective our H&H culture. You will lead the collection and analysis of people data to generate actionable insights that inform strategic workforce planning and decision-making. The role blends strategy with execution: developing and implementing systems, policies, and processes while managing both operational and strategic elements of the reward, benefits, and recognition function. This is a unique opportunity to make a visible impact on H&H’s people strategy, influence the employee experience, and drive initiatives that attract, retain, and engage top talent across the ANZ region.




Key responsibilities include: 

Plan, lead, and execute cyclical total rewards activities, including annual remuneration review, bonuses, budget forecasting, and collaboration with HRBPs and managers  

Lead salary benchmarking and market pay analysis, ensuring competitiveness and equity using external data and provider insights  

Advise leaders and HR stakeholders on total rewards strategies, including remuneration, benefits, recognition, policies, and standards  

Design, manage, and evolve employee benefits and recognition programs, enhancing effectiveness, accessibility, engagement, and alignment with EVP and experience initiatives  

Produce data-driven people insights, analysing internal and external data to inform workforce planning, DE&I initiatives, and strategic HR decisions  

Maintain and enhance reporting systems, dashboards, and governance to ensure accurate, timely information supporting planning, forecasting, compliance, and HR strategic projects  

Support DE&I initiatives, including gender equity, scorecards, WGEA reporting, and tracking progress across the business  

Oversee talent mobility, ensuring compliance with business guidelines and managing vendor relationships for immigration, relocation, and advisory services  

Monitor industry trends, legislative updates, and best practices to continuously optimise total rewards programs and enhance employee experience




Desired Skills & Experience 

We look for people who have what it takes to make an impact; who can challenge us to go further while pushing themselves to deliver exciting & ambitious results.  

To make sure we’re setting new starters up for success, we ask that you meet a few criteria relevant to the role to be considered for this opportunity: 

Relevant degree (e.g., Business, Management, Economics) or equivalent experience in remuneration, reward, or people analytics  

5+ years’ experience in a similar corporate role managing compensation, benefits, or total rewards, preferably in Australia or Asia Pacific  

Strong understanding of reward and benefits strategies, legislation, and performance linkages  

Experience in job evaluation, salary benchmarking, and pay determination based on market factors  

Proven ability to design and optimise total rewards programs, including innovative reward initiatives  

Skilled in systems, processes, and project delivery, including system integration (SIT) and user acceptance testing (UAT)  

Advanced skills in cost modelling, strategic analysis, data visualization, and HR reporting  

Expertise in analysing people data to generate actionable insights for workforce planning and decision-making  

Knowledge of external salary and benefits surveys, compliance reporting, and regulatory frameworks (e.g., WGEA)  

Demonstrated ability to combine analytical rigor with strategic thinking to influence business and HR stakeholders

Job Title: Senior Manager – GRC Tooling & Risk Management
Ad Text:
Our Client is looking for a proactive Senior Manager to join their Technology & Program Risk practice. This position is ideal for a strategic, systems-based thinker with a strong background in either GRC system implementation, business analysis, and/or technology risk management. You will lead and oversee a small team of staff and be part of the leadership team in this impressive consulting firm. You will be working with top-tier clients, giving you exposure to large-scale risk transformation programs across ASX50 clients in Financial Services, Industry and Government.

The focus of this position is advising clients on GRC tooling architecture, configuration, and functional design, ensuring solutions align with both regulatory, risk and business obligations. The engagements you will lead will be in GRC platform implementation, such as Archer, OpenPages Protecht, ReadiNow, SAI360 or ServiceNow.

Key Responsibilities
- Lead large-scale risk transformation and GRC tooling programs across a range of industries, covering Risk management, Compliance, Internal audit, Business Continuity Management, Vendor and Third-Party Risk.
- Advise stakeholders throughout system implementation projects by defining business and regulatory requirements and translating requirements into robust, scalable solution designs aligned with industry best practice.
- Review and evaluate existing client GRC implementations to identify opportunities for uplift, optimisation, or reversion to out-of-the-box functionality.
- Design and deliver reports, dashboards, and analytics that enhance risk insights and decision-making.
- Stay informed on emerging technology risks, regulatory developments, and industry trends.
- Lead and coach a team of Consultants.

Qualifications & Experience
- Degree with professional certifications in either CBAP, CISA, CISSP, CISM, CRISC, PMP, Agile, PmBok or PRINCE2 are a plus.
- 5+ years’ experience in Technology Risk, GRC, Program Audit or Assurance or business analysis, with demonstrated involvement in GRC system implementations.
- Experience gained in the Big 4 or the Consulting environment and / or enterprise environments are preferred.
- Exposure to GRC platforms such as Archer, OpenPages, Protecht, ReadiNow, SAI360 and / or ServiceNow is needed.
- Strong understanding of risk and control frameworks such as ISO 27001, CPS 234, COBIT, NIST etc.

Ready to grow your GRC career in this impressive consulting firm while working on meaningful, complex engagements? Apply now and be part of a team shaping the future of Technology Risk and Governance!  

📞Tania Barnard on 0411 018 788 or e-mail taniab@ethosbc.com.au

At Saputo, we bring good to the table by making high-quality products, investing in our people, and supporting communities around the world . As a top 10 global dairy processor, we value contributions that matter and strive to foster an inclusive, growth-driven work environment. Ready to bring your best?

About the Role

We’re recruiting a Senior Manager Commercial Finance – Projects & Marketing to join our Domestic Commercial Finance leadership team at Saputo Dairy Australia. This is a senior commercial partnering role, working closely with the General Manager of Marketing & Foodservice, with accountability for strengthening the commercial evaluation and governance of major initiatives across the domestic consumer business. You will lead the financial evaluation of new product development and marketing initiatives, support Brand Planning and Portfolio Review processes and provide rigorous analysis and recommendations for major commercial contracts.

Reporting to the Head of Commercial Finance – Domestic, you will lead and coach Project Finance Leads/Analysts, ensuring consistent evaluation frameworks, strong cross‑functional collaboration, and high‑quality end‑to‑end assessments that enable confident decision‑making at senior forums. This is not a delivery or project management role — it’s a role for a leader who thrives in ambiguity, applies strong commercial judgement, and influences outcomes through insight, challenge and structured evaluation.

Key Responsibilities

Lead the financial evaluation and governance of major initiatives, ensuring clear commercial insights, scenarios, and recommendations support senior decision‑making.

Oversee product and project build evaluations across categories, ensuring robust, consistent modelling frameworks and transparent methodologies are applied end‑to‑end.

Partner with Marketing, Category and Commercial teams through Brand Planning and Portfolio Review processes, embedding risks, opportunities and financial insights into strategic decisions.

Lead the review, analysis and recommendation of major commercial contracts and agreements, applying strong financial rigour aligned to business strategy.

Lead, coach and uplift capability across Project Finance Leads/Analysts, driving consistency of approach, continuous improvement, and strong cross‑functional partnering.

About You

Extensive experience (typically 10+ years) in commercial finance, financial evaluation or business partnering roles within FMCG or similarly complex environments.

CA / CPA qualified; project management qualification desirable but not essential.

Strong financial and economic modelling capability, with the ability to translate complex analysis into clear, actionable recommendations.

Demonstrated experience leading financial assessments for large‑scale initiatives, contracts, or portfolio decisions and influencing senior stakeholders.

Confident operating in ambiguity and applying commercial judgement to challenge assumptions and improve decision quality.

Proven people leadership capability, including coaching and uplifting consistency and quality across a team.

What We Offer

A senior commercial finance leadership role with high visibility across major decisions, initiatives and governance forums.

Opportunity to influence how the business evaluates and commits to growth investments, supported by established evaluation frameworks.

Broad cross‑functional exposure across Marketing, Category, R&D, PMO and Commercial teams in a complex FMCG environment.

The Role

We are on the lookout for a Senior Manager – Data/AI Governance to lead the development and rollout of enterprise governance practices, with data governance at the core.

This role is fundamentally focused on establishing strong data governance foundations—ensuring data is accurate, secure, well-managed, and compliant. Building on this, you will help shape how AI is governed across the organisation, recognising that effective AI governance depends on high-quality, well-governed data.

You’ll operate in a hands-on capacity initially, with the opportunity to build out a team over time. Working across business, technology, and risk, you’ll embed governance into day-to-day operations and enable the organisation to scale its data and AI capabilities safely.

Main Responsibilities
Design and implement data governance frameworks, policies, and standards as the foundation for broader AI governance
Embed governance across the full data lifecycle
Establish/maintain data controls aligned with regulatory/risk requirements
Drive data quality improvements through monitoring/analysis/ remediation
Partner with business units and data owners to strengthen accountability and ownership
Support AI and model governance, ensuring alignment with core data governance principles
Implement governance tooling
Collaborate with risk, legal, and tech teams to embed governance practices
Contribute to governance forums and senior stakeholder reporting
Support vendor and delivery governance standards
Key Skills & Experience
Strong experience in data governance as a core discipline 
Exposure to AI governance or model risk 
Proven background in regulated or complex environments
Deep understanding of data lifecycle, quality, and compliance frameworks
Experience implementing governance frameworks across business/tech teams
Ability to influence stakeholders and drive governance practices
Ability to lead a team
Exposure to AI/ML or model governance and data platforms would be an asset

Ad Text:
Drive meaningful transformation by delivering expert business analysis, process improvement and project support across BAI’s enterprise portfolio.




WHY BAI COMMUNICATIONS

It’s an exciting time to be part of BAI Communications. Building on our achievements in broadcasting, telecommunications and critical communications, we continue to expand our operations, helping connect people and support communities.

 

We operate one of the world’s most extensive broadcast networks, delivering fully managed television and radio services to 99% of the Australian population. During crises such as natural disasters, national broadcasters depend on BAI to maintain vital communications, while emergency services trust us to keep them connected and informed.




As 5G technology transforms connectivity, BAI is leveraging global experience to enable comprehensive coverage in challenging environments—buildings, tunnels, stadiums, and high-density areas. This expertise positions us at the forefront of intelligent, connected infrastructure development across Australia.




Our acquisition of Titan ICT has strengthened our mission-critical capabilities, adding deep expertise in mining, resources, and energy industries. This expansion enables us to deploy robust solutions across Australia’s most demanding operational environments.




Discover more through BAI360, our virtual tour platform. 

 

LET’S EXPLORE THE ROLE

As a Business Analyst – Transformation, you will play a key role in delivering BAI’s enterprise transformation agenda. Working across multiple initiatives, you will partner with business leaders, project teams and technical specialists to analyse complex requirements, simplify processes and ensure transformation initiatives deliver measurable value.




This is a hands-on, adaptable role suited to someone who thrives in a fast-paced environment, can balance multiple priorities, and brings strong relationship‑building skills. In addition to core business analysis, you’ll provide flexible support across test management and change management activities as required to ensure successful project delivery.

 

Key Responsibilities:

Lead a variety of business analysis activities across transformation initiatives
Elicit, document and manage high-quality business, functional and non-functional requirements
Map current and future state processes, identifying opportunities for simplification, automation and efficiency
Facilitate workshops, stakeholder interviews and requirements sessions to drive shared understanding and buy-in
Manage requirements throughout the project lifecycle, ensuring alignment with business objectives and delivery constraints
Provide flexible project support, contributing to test management and change management activities where needed
Champion continuous improvement across business processes and transformation outcomes
Produce clear, compliant documentation including process maps, user stories, test artefacts and project deliverables
Provide regular insights and reporting on progress, risks, dependencies and outcomes




LET’S HEAR ABOUT YOU

You are an experienced Business Analyst with a strong background in transformation and complex project environments. You combine analytical rigour with strong people skills and are confident working across diverse stakeholder groups.

You bring expert capability in business analysis and process improvement, along with advanced facilitation, documentation and stakeholder engagement skills. You’re adaptable, solutions-focused and comfortable contributing beyond traditional BA boundaries to support successful delivery.

 

Technical Skills Required:

Strong command of business analysis and end-to-end requirements management
Highly developed capability in process mapping, analysis and continuous improvement
Demonstrated strength in stakeholder engagement, workshop design and facilitation
Proven proficiency using Microsoft Visio, Excel and the Microsoft Office Suite
Well-established experience eliciting requirements, developing user stories and defining acceptance criteria
Hands-on experience with PowerBI and data visualisation techniques
Practical experience working across Agile and hybrid delivery methodologies
Sound experience supporting test management, UAT coordination and change management activities
Solid working knowledge of enterprise architecture principles, ITIL frameworks and system implementation concepts




BENEFITS & CULTURE

...

About the Catholic Development Fund

Catholic Development Fund (CDF) are a values-based financial institution that provides funding and support across its footprint of Catholic organisations, including parishes, primary and secondary schools, healthcare, aged care, and social services providers.

Our work enables real impact: from hospital wings for the sick, safe havens for the homeless, and classrooms for the young – we support our customers to grow their mission. Through our expertise and commitment, we help Catholic organisations put their faith into action and contribute to a fairer, more cohesive society.

About the Role

We're seeking a creative and motivated Process Improvement Specialist, Retail to join our Process Transformation team. This is a pivotal role focused on transforming how our clients interact with our Retail Fund, CDF Community Fund – from their first enquiry through to ongoing engagement and retention.

The Process Improvement Specialist will work closely with the Client Experience Specialist and is responsible for implementing initiatives and championing continuous improvement to enhance the end-to-end Community Fund experience. The role focuses on ownership of forms and processes, automation, data quality uplift, regulatory compliance, CRM readiness, and system optimisation to support organisational transformation and ensure a seamless, scalable client journey.

Key Accountabilities

Community Fund Process Optimisation

Support and implement the full applicant-to-onboarding workflow to reduce manual effort, improve transparency, and enhance client experience.
Implement automation opportunities (forms, workflows, notifications, approvals) across the Community Fund lifecycle.
Map and document current-state and future-state procedures and processes.
Partner with Marketing, Client Services, Data, and IT teams to implement process enhancements.

CRM Migration Support – Client Data and Process Readiness

Assess, cleanse, and structure Community Fund client data to ensure readiness for Dynamics 365 CRM migration.
Develop data quality rules, validation logic, and remediation steps for legacy datasets.
Partner with Data and IT and CRM squads to embed process logic, workflow requirements, and business rules into the CRM design.

KYC Compliance and Risk Management

Support the Executive Manager Retail in a KYC uplift program for the Community Fund, addressing non-compliance with existing client records.
Design improved KYC collection, verification, and evidence workflows aligned to regulatory obligations.
Implement sustainable mechanisms to maintain ongoing compliance through digital capture and automated reminders.

Core Banking Platform – Functionality Optimisation

Analyse current use of core banking platform functionality as it relates to the Community Fund.
Identify opportunities to streamline tasks, reduce manual workarounds, and leverage underused features.
Work with vendors and internal product teams to test and implement improvements.

Reporting, Insights & Performance Measurement

Track key process performance metrics including processing times, data quality, KYC completion, and automation effectiveness.
Provide regular insights and recommendations to the Executive Manager, Process Transformation.
Support continuous improvement cycles with data-driven analysis.

Cross-Functional Collaboration

Partner with Marketing and Community Engagement teams on client-focused initiatives.
Collaborate with Relationship Managers to understand client needs and enhance service delivery.
Work with Risk and Compliance teams to ensure client processes meet regulatory requirements.

What You Bring

Essential

Demonstrated experience in process improvement, business analysis, or transformation roles.
Strong capability in process mapping, optimisation, and automation design.
Experience working with CRM systems (Dynamics 365 preferred) and data cleansing/migration activities.
Understanding of regulatory compliance frameworks including KYC/AML.
Ability to engage diverse stakeholders and simplify complex concepts.
Strong digital literacy and experience implementing digital-first solutions.
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills.

At Acenda Group, everything we do is driven by a clear purpose: to help our customers and their families live, save and retire with confidence.

We bring together some of Australia and New Zealand’s most trusted life insurance brands. With over 176 years of combined heritage and deep expertise across our local markets, our Group includes Acenda Life, Resolution Life Australasia, and Asteron Life New Zealand.

Backed by the global strength of Nippon Life, we are one of the largest life insurance businesses across Australia and New Zealand. More than two million customers rely on us for life insurance, wealth protection and retirement solutions that support their confidence at every stage of life.

We’re building an inclusive, dynamic and future focused business. Joining us means stepping into a period of integration and growth, where your work will help shape our future.

If you’re energised by change and motivated by purpose, this is your moment to make an impact.

The role

Reporting into the Head of Digital, the Digital Enablement and Experience Lead is a newly created role within the function.

You will be responsible for uplifting Acenda’s enterprise digital capability across platforms, portals, digital experiences and end‑to‑end customer, adviser and partner journeys.

As we transition from a fragmented multi‑portal landscape to a unified, scalable and efficient digital environment, you will continue to drive improvements to the customer journey experience across our Group, Retail and Retirement lines.

You will be responsible for safeguarding the continuity and simplification of our digital assets, enhancing user experiences, and enabling sustainable growth across all channels and propositions.

About you

An experienced Digital Manager, you possess a minimum of 7+ years of broad digital experiences across MarTech, digital enablement and experience design within the financial services sector.

You have a proven track record in enabling and delivering tangible customer journey improvements across enterprise digital platforms, apps, and portals in regulated environments.

You ideally have been involved in projects or possess prior experience in consolidating digital estates, overseeing platform migrations and standardisation whilst maintaining and optimising operational capabilities.

Lastly, you possess strong influencing and leadership skills with the ability to lead multi-disciplinary digital teams in a hybrid and agile delivery environment.

Join us in building confidence— for our customers, our communities and each other.

·       Your work makes a real impact. Everything we do helps our customers and their families live, save and retire with confidence, and your contribution will directly support that purpose.

·       You’ll help drive meaningful change. As an inclusive, dynamic and adaptable organisation, we offer a unique opportunity for you to grow while shaping the future of our organisation.

·       You’ll feel supported and included. Our team culture is flexible, collaborative and people‑centred, with hybrid working to help you balance life and work.

·       By making things simpler. We help our people operate efficiently, adapt easily and spend more time on meaningful work that drives personal and organisational growth.

·       We celebrate success. Our incentive and recognition programs ensure great work is acknowledged and rewarded.

·       Your wellbeing comes first. We offer tools, resources and benefits that support your whole self, including additional lifestyle leave.

We celebrate the diversity of our customers and community, and we embed diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging into how we do business. We encourage applications from people of all backgrounds, cultures, experiences, and identities.

Should an applicant be the preferred candidate, background checks (including Federal Police Checks, Employment checks, ASIC banned and disqualified persons and Bankruptcy checks) will be completed prior to the candidate's employment being confirmed. The outcomes of the background checks do not automatically bar candidates, however they will be assessed against the inherent requirements of the position.

You will work with the insurance management to ensure GPT's insurance program is effectively managed, risks are appropriately mitigated, and compliance standards are maintained across all assets and entities, while implementing efficiencies to streamline responsibilities and deliverables.

Job Title: Senior Data Engineer / Senior Analytics Engineer
Ad Text:
Senior Data Engineer

At Elula, we partner with some of Australia's top financial institutions to tackle a wide range of business challenges through cutting-edge AI-as-a-service (AIaaS). Our vision? To make the complex simple and easy, delivering amazing customer experiences and personalisation that matters.

Our products leverage machine learning, productionised models, data pipelines, explainable AI, and rich visualisations to drive customer engagement, retention and insights. We're constantly pushing the envelope, uncovering the ever-evolving needs of customers and staying ahead of the curve.

Join a team of some of the brightest minds in data science and engineering; experts with years of experience and a passion for delivering smarter decision-making. It's a fast-paced, high-performing and collaborative environment where creativity and learning take centre stage, and the work is as fun as it is rewarding.

Find out more about us at https://elula.ai/team/.




The role

At Elula, data is the foundation of our AI-as-a-Service platform. As a Senior Data Engineer, you'll be the person who truly understands that data: where it comes from, what it means, how it behaves, and how to shape it into something reliable and useful.

Most of your best work will happen before you write any code; in understanding what the data represents, where it came from, and how it fits into our products. You'll spend your time interrogating complex datasets, designing thoughtful data models, and building the transformation logic that turns raw inputs into trusted, high-quality outputs powering our models and products. You won't just move data - you'll make sense of it.

You'll work autonomously on open-ended problems alongside data scientists, software engineers, and customer-facing teams, contributing across the full data lifecycle from ingestion and modelling through to validation and delivery. We're a collaborative, ambitious team, and we're looking for someone who takes genuine ownership, communicates clearly across disciplines, and isn't afraid to ask the hard questions about the data in front of them.

To be successful in this role, you will be expected to demonstrate the following skills and experience.
 

What You’ll Bring

Data transformation and modelling

A deep curiosity for data - you naturally interrogate datasets to understand their shape, quality, and fitness for purpose before building anything.

Strong applied data modelling skills, designing structures that serve analytical, operational and downstream use cases well.

The ability to develop and optimise complex SQL across large datasets, with a sharp eye for correctness, performance and long-term maintainability.

Comfortable using Python to automate workflows and building reusable tooling that makes the team more effective.

Ownership and judgement

You own your work end-to-end, from design decisions through to ongoing reliability, exercising sound technical judgement when trade-offs arise and proactively keeping stakeholders informed.

You manage your own priorities and delivery with discipline, and help your team do the same without waiting to be asked.

When something's wrong with the data, you dig in, diagnose it systematically, and fix it pragmatically, then share what you learned so the same issue doesn't come back.

 Technical leadership

You communicate complex data concepts clearly to both technical colleagues and non-technical stakeholders - translating between the two comes naturally.

You actively support and uplift junior engineers, sharing context, approaches and best practices generously.

You bring curiosity and a continuous improvement mindset, staying across new tools and techniques and contributing to raising the bar for the whole team.

You know how to get the most out of AI-assisted development tools like Claude Code - using them as an amplifier to think bigger and spend your energy on problems that genuinely require human judgment.




Nice to Have

While the following are not required, they are highly valued.

A background in a quantitative discipline such as computer science, mathematics or statistics.

Experience working in the financial services industry (e.g. banking, insurance or superannuation)


Benefits

Elula’s people are at the core of all we do. Our benefits are just one way we show that.

Enjoy flexible working hours, and work from home two days per week, to better balance your personal and professional life.

Experience the freedom of working anywhere in Australia up to five days annually.

Give back to what matters to you with extra paid annual leave for volunteering.

Put your health first with an allowance for fitness and wellbeing.

Drive your development with financial support for approved training courses.

Level up with matched funding for career coaching.




Interested?

If you're excited about shaping the future of AI, this is where you belong! Submit your CV and cover letter telling us why you're the right fit for Elula and what makes you special!

We're committed to fostering a culture of inclusivity and equality. All are welcome to apply for our roles because we truly believe that diversity is a game changer.

Job Title: Senior Data Governance Lead (12 months Contract) (Melb, Syd, Hobart, Brisbane)
Ad Text:
About us
At CareSuper, we believe we’re Super Together. As a profit-to-member super fund with over 600,000 members and $60 billion in assets, we’re passionate about helping our members retire with confidence. We’re growing fast and looking for our next superhero to join us on this exciting journey. 

About the role
As our new Senior Data Governance Lead (12‑month fixed term), you'll be part of the Data and Information Governance function within Technology Delivery. Reporting to the Manager, Data and Information Governance, this role plays a critical hands‑on role in uplifting the fund's data quality, controls, and governance platforms.

This is a technically‑oriented role focused on delivery rather than policy writing. You'll work closely with data engineering, analytics, risk, and platform teams to embed practical, evidence‑based data governance across the organisation.

If successful, a day in the life will look like the below;

Leading the end‑to‑end execution of the Data Quality program, including profiling, controls, monitoring, and remediation
Configuring and operating InvestigateDQ, translating business and governance requirements into effective, SQL‑based data quality rules and controls
Providing hands‑on leadership across data quality controls, governance metrics, and assurance evidence
Configuring and supporting Microsoft Purview for metadata management, classification, lineage, and governance workflows
Partnering with data engineering teams to implement and embed data governance controls within Databricks
Leading and supporting a small team, providing technical direction and prioritisation 
Producing clear, defensible governance metrics, reporting, and evidence to support internal governance and assurance activities
Working closely with stakeholders across Technology, Risk, Compliance, and Analytics to ensure governance controls are practical, adopted, and effective
What we're looking for
We're on the lookout for a technically capable data governance practitioner who enjoys applying governance principles directly within modern data platforms.
You'll thrive in this role if you have:

Strong experience in data quality management, including profiling, rules, controls, monitoring, and remediation
Practical experience working with SQL‑based data quality rules and controls, and confidence engaging directly with data
Hands‑on experience configuring or operating data governance or data quality platforms (InvestigateDQ or similar strongly preferred)
Experience working with Microsoft Purview or similar metadata / cataloguing tools 
A solid understanding of how data governance controls are implemented within cloud data platforms (e.g. Databricks)
The ability to lead technically, mentor others, and prioritise delivery 
Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate governance requirements into practical technical outcomes
A pragmatic mindset — focused on uplift, evidence, and execution
Exposure to automation or API‑based integration with governance tools
Experience supporting governance uplift in regulated environments
Why CareSuper?
We're not just another super fund. We're a values‑driven organisation that puts people first — our members and our team.
Here's what you can look forward to:

12‑month fixed‑term opportunity with meaningful delivery impact
Hybrid and flexible work environment
3% Superannuation Guarantee (SG) above industry minimum
Matching additional superannuation salary sacrifice of up to 2%
5 weeks of annual leave
17 weeks of parental leave for all eligible employees
Holistic health and wellbeing support
Professional development and external coaching
Rewards and recognition program and employee benefits
Ready to apply? 
We’d love to hear from you! Just send through your resume and a cover letter. Be quick—we may close applications early if we find the right person. 

What happens next? 
Need support during the application or interview process? We’re here to help. Just reach out to your talent partner and let us know how we can make the process work for you. 

We’re committed to protecting your privacy—your personal information will be handled with care and in line with our Privacy Policy. 

At CareSuper, we know that diverse perspectives make us stronger. We’re proud to foster an inclusive environment where everyone feels welcome, respected, and valued—no matter your background or identity.
